0.1: The Lord's teaching to the nations through the twelve apostles.
1.1: There are two ways: one of life and one of death, and there is a great difference between the two ways.
1.2: The way of life is this: first, love God who made you; second, love your neighbor as yourself; and whatever you would not want done to you, do not do to anyone else.
1.3: And the teaching of these words is this: bless those who curse you, pray for your enemies, and fast for those who persecute you. For what credit is it if you love those who love you? Do not the nations do the same? But you — love those who hate you, and you will have no enemy.
1.4: Keep away from fleshly and bodily cravings. If someone slaps your right cheek, turn the other to him as well, and you will be perfect. If someone forces you to go one mile, go with him two. If someone takes your coat, give him your shirt too. If someone takes what is yours, do not ask for it back — you could not anyway.
1.5: Give to everyone who asks you, and do not ask for it back, for the Father wants gifts given to all from what is his own. Blessed is the one who gives as the commandment says, for he is innocent. But woe to the one who takes: if he takes because he is in need, he is innocent; but if he takes without needing it, he will have to answer for why he took it and what for. He will be locked up and questioned about what he did, and he will not get out until he has paid back the last penny.
1.6: But about this it has also been said: let your gift sweat in your hands until you know whom you are giving it to.
2.1: The second commandment of the teaching is this:
2.2: Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not corrupt boys, do not be sexually immoral, do not steal, do not practice magic, do not use sorcery, do not kill a child by abortion or kill it once it is born, do not covet what belongs to your neighbor.
2.3: Do not break an oath, do not give false testimony, do not slander, do not hold a grudge.
2.4: Do not be two-faced or two-tongued, for a double tongue is a deadly trap.
2.5: Your word must not be false or empty, but backed up by action.
2.6: Do not be greedy or grasping or a hypocrite or spiteful or arrogant. Do not plot evil against your neighbor.
2.7: Do not hate anyone; some you should correct, some you should pray for, and some you should love more than your own life.
3.1: My child, run from everything evil and from anything like it.
3.2: Do not be quick to anger, for anger leads to murder; do not be jealous or quarrelsome or hot-tempered, for all of these breed murder.
3.3: My child, do not be lustful, for lust leads to sexual immorality; do not be foul-mouthed or let your eyes wander, for all of these breed adultery.
3.4: My child, do not read omens, since it leads to idolatry; do not cast spells or study the stars or perform rituals of purification, and do not even want to watch such things, for all of these breed idolatry.
3.5: My child, do not be a liar, since lying leads to theft; do not love money or chase empty glory, for all of these breed theft.
3.6: My child, do not be a grumbler, since it leads to slander against God; do not be stubborn or evil-minded, for all of these breed blasphemy.
3.7: Instead, be gentle, for the gentle will inherit the earth.
3.8: Be patient, merciful, harmless, peaceable, and good, and always hold in awe the words you have heard.
3.9: Do not think highly of yourself or let yourself grow brazen. Do not keep company with the proud, but live among the righteous and humble.
3.10: Accept whatever happens to you as good, knowing that nothing happens apart from God.
4.1: My child, remember night and day the one who speaks God's word to you, and honor him as you would the Lord; for wherever the Lord's nature is spoken of, there the Lord is.
4.2: Seek out every day the company of the holy ones, so that you may find rest in their words.
4.3: Do not stir up division, but make peace between those who quarrel. Judge fairly, and do not show favoritism when you correct wrongdoing.
4.4: Do not waver over whether something will be or not.
4.5: Do not be someone who reaches out his hands to take but pulls them back when it is time to give.
4.6: If you have earned anything with your hands, give some of it to pay for your sins.
4.7: Do not hesitate to give, and do not grumble when you give, for you will learn who the good rewarder is.
4.8: Do not turn away the needy, but share everything with your brother and do not say that anything is your own; for if you share in what is immortal, how much more in what is mortal?
4.9: Do not withhold your hand from your son or your daughter, but from their youth teach them the fear of God.
4.10: Do not give harsh orders, in your bitterness, to your servant or maid, who hope in the same God, or they may stop fearing the God who is over you both; for he does not call people by their rank, but those whom the Spirit has prepared.
4.11: And you servants, submit to your masters as you would to God, with respect and reverence.
4.12: Hate all hypocrisy and everything that does not please the Lord.
4.13: Do not abandon the Lord's commandments, but keep what you were given, neither adding to it nor taking from it.
4.14: In the assembly confess your wrongs, and do not come to pray with a guilty conscience. This is the way of life.
5.1: But the way of death is this: above all it is evil and full of curse — murders, adulteries, lusts, sexual immorality, thefts, idolatries, magic arts, sorceries, robberies, false testimony, hypocrisy, a divided heart, deceit, arrogance, malice, stubbornness, greed, filthy talk, jealousy, recklessness, pride, and boasting.
5.2: They persecute good people, hate the truth, and love lies; they do not recognize the reward of righteousness; they do not hold to what is good or to fair judgment; they stay alert not for good but for evil. Gentleness and patience are far from them. They love worthless things, chase after payment, show no pity to the poor, do not work for the oppressed, and do not know the one who made them. They are murderers of children and destroyers of what God has made; they turn away the needy and crush the afflicted; they defend the rich but are unjust judges of the poor — utterly sinful. May you be rescued, children, from all of these.
6.1: See that no one leads you away from this way of the teaching, since such a person would be teaching you without God.
6.2: For if you can carry the whole yoke of the Lord, you will be perfect; but if you cannot, do as much as you can.
6.3: And about food, take on what you can; but stay well away from what is offered to idols, for that is the worship of dead gods.
7.1: About baptism, baptize this way: after you have gone over all these things, baptize in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, in running water.
7.2: If you have no running water, baptize in other water; and if you cannot in cold, then in warm.
7.3: If you have neither, pour water on the head three times in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit.
7.4: Before the baptism, the one baptizing and the one being baptized should fast, along with any others who can; and tell the one being baptized to fast for a day or two beforehand.
8.1: Do not let your fasts be like the hypocrites', for they fast on Monday and Thursday; you fast instead on Wednesday and Friday.
8.2: And do not pray like the hypocrites, but as the Lord commanded in his gospel, pray this way: Our Father in heaven, may your name be honored as holy, may your kingdom come, may your will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us today our daily bread, and forgive us our debt, as we also forgive those in debt to us, and do not bring us into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one; for yours is the power and the glory forever.
8.3: Pray this way three times a day.
9.1: About the thanksgiving meal, give thanks this way.
9.2: First, over the cup: We thank you, our Father, for the holy vine of David your servant, which you made known to us through Jesus your servant. To you be glory forever.
9.3: And over the broken bread: We thank you, our Father, for the life and knowledge that you made known to us through Jesus your servant. To you be glory forever.
9.4: As this broken bread was scattered over the hills and then gathered together to become one, so may your church be gathered from the ends of the earth into your kingdom; for yours is the glory and the power through Jesus Christ forever.
9.5: And let no one eat or drink of your thanksgiving meal except those baptized in the name of the Lord; for about this the Lord also said, "Do not give what is holy to the dogs."
10.1: After the meal, give thanks this way:
10.2: We thank you, holy Father, for your holy name, which you have made to dwell in our hearts, and for the knowledge and faith and immortality that you made known to us through Jesus your servant. To you be glory forever.
10.3: You, almighty Master, created all things for the sake of your name; you gave food and drink to people to enjoy, so that they would thank you — but to us you have freely given spiritual food and drink and eternal life through your servant.
10.4: Above all we thank you because you are mighty. To you be glory forever.
10.5: Remember, Lord, your church: deliver it from all evil and make it perfect in your love, and gather it — made holy — from the four winds into your kingdom, which you have prepared for it; for yours is the power and the glory forever.
10.6: Let grace come, and let this world pass away. Hosanna to the God of David! If anyone is holy, let him come; if anyone is not, let him repent. Maranatha. Amen.
10.7: But let the prophets give thanks as much as they wish.
11.1: So whoever comes and teaches you all these things said above, welcome him.
11.2: But if the teacher himself turns and teaches a different teaching meant to tear this down, do not listen to him; but if his teaching adds righteousness and knowledge of the Lord, welcome him as the Lord.
11.3: And about the apostles and prophets, act according to the rule of the gospel.
11.4: Let every apostle who comes to you be welcomed as the Lord.
11.5: But he must not stay more than one day, or two if there is need; but if he stays three, he is a false prophet.
11.6: And when the apostle leaves, let him take nothing but bread until he reaches his next lodging; but if he asks for money, he is a false prophet.
11.7: And do not test or judge any prophet who speaks in the spirit; for every sin will be forgiven, but this sin will not be forgiven.
11.8: But not everyone who speaks in the spirit is a prophet — only the one who has the ways of the Lord. So the false prophet and the true prophet will be known by their ways.
11.9: And any prophet who orders a meal in the spirit shall not eat of it himself; if he does, he is a false prophet.
11.10: And every prophet who teaches the truth but does not do what he teaches is a false prophet.
11.11: But every proven, true prophet who acts out the earthly mystery of the church, yet does not teach others to do what he himself does, is not to be judged by you; for his judgment rests with God, since the ancient prophets acted the same way.
11.12: But whoever says in the spirit, "Give me money" or anything else, do not listen to him; but if he asks you to give for others who are in need, let no one judge him.
12.1: Welcome everyone who comes in the name of the Lord; then examine him and you will find out what he is, for you will have the sense to tell right from wrong.
12.2: If the one coming is just passing through, help him as much as you can; but he must not stay with you more than two or three days, if there is need.
12.3: But if he wants to settle among you and has a trade, let him work and eat.
12.4: But if he has no trade, use your good sense to see that he does not live among you as an idle Christian.
12.5: But if he refuses to do this, he is trading on Christ. Watch out for people like that.
13.1: But every true prophet who wants to settle among you is worthy of his food.
13.2: In the same way a true teacher is worthy, like the worker, of his food.
13.3: So take every firstfruit of the produce of the winepress and threshing floor, and of cattle and sheep, and give the firstfruit to the prophets; for they are your high priests.
13.4: But if you have no prophet, give it to the poor.
13.5: If you make bread, take the firstfruit and give it as the commandment says.
13.6: In the same way, when you open a jar of wine or oil, take the firstfruit and give it to the prophets.
13.7: And of money and clothing and every possession, take the firstfruit, as seems right to you, and give it as the commandment says.
14.1: On the Lord's own day, gather together, break bread, and give thanks, after first confessing your sins, so that your sacrifice may be pure.
14.2: But let no one who has a quarrel with his companion join you until they are reconciled, so that your sacrifice may not be defiled.
14.3: For this is what was spoken by the Lord: In every place and time offer me a pure sacrifice, for I am a great king, says the Lord, and my name is wonderful among the nations.
15.1: So choose for yourselves overseers and deacons worthy of the Lord — gentle men, not lovers of money, truthful and proven; for they too carry out for you the work of the prophets and teachers.
15.2: So do not look down on them, for they are the honored ones among you, along with the prophets and teachers.
15.3: Correct one another, not in anger but in peace, as you have it in the gospel; and if anyone wrongs another, let no one speak to him, and let him hear nothing from you, until he repents.
15.4: And do your prayers and your giving and everything you do as you have it in the gospel of our Lord.
16.1: Watch over your life. Do not let your lamps go out or your belts come loose, but be ready, for you do not know the hour when our Lord is coming.
16.2: Gather together often, seeking what is good for your souls; for all the time of your faith will be of no use to you unless you are made perfect at the last moment.
16.3: For in the last days the false prophets and the corrupters will multiply, and the sheep will be turned into wolves, and love will be turned into hatred.
16.4: For as lawlessness increases, people will hate one another and persecute and betray; and then the deceiver of the world will appear as a son of God, and will do signs and wonders, and the earth will be handed over into his hands, and he will commit outrages such as have never happened from the beginning of time.
16.5: Then humankind will come into the fire of testing, and many will fall away and perish; but those who stand firm in their faith will be saved by the curse itself.
16.6: And then the signs of the truth will appear: first the sign of an opening in heaven, then the sign of a trumpet's sound, and third the resurrection of the dead —
16.7: but not of everyone; rather, as it was said: The Lord will come, and all the holy ones with him.
16.8: Then the world will see the Lord coming on the clouds of heaven.
